p1_string_of_nickels.physical_facts: Yellow pot, dense foliage, stable orientation.
p1_string_of_nickels.explanatory_transformations: Remains consistent throughout the sequence; no significant morphological shifts observed.
p1_string_of_nickels.visual_health_reasoning: Healthy; turgor pressure appears maintained despite VPD stress.

p2_mexican_mint.physical_facts: Black pot, central position, two primary leaves.
p2_mexican_mint.explanatory_transformations: The plant shows signs of recovery from the initial drooping observed in the earliest image. The leaf margins are stabilizing.
p2_mexican_mint.visual_health_reasoning: Improving; the reduction in visible dehydration suggests the recent starch water application is aiding moisture retention.

p3_pothos.physical_facts: Black pot, two leaves, white rabbit reference anchor.
p3_pothos.explanatory_transformations: The leaf necrosis noted in previous reports remains static; no further progression of tissue death.
p3_pothos.visual_health_reasoning: Stable; the plant is holding its current state without further decline.

p4_silver_guest.physical_facts: Smallest specimen, located near the rim of the black pot.
p4_silver_guest.explanatory_transformations: Shows slight improvement in posture compared to the earliest image where it appeared more desiccated.
p4_silver_guest.visual_health_reasoning: Recovering; the recent hydration intervention has visibly improved the leaf turgidity.

biome_observations.soil_surface: Presence of white starch residue confirms the user's action of applying starch water. Soil moisture appears adequate across all pots.
biome_observations.incidental_growth: No new weeds or uncatalogued sprouts detected.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: None; the white particulate matter is identified as the starch-based supplement.

vision_report.temporal_deltas: The sequence shows a transition from a state of visible dehydration (earliest image) to a stabilized, hydrated state following the 2026-04-09 intervention.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The garden is currently in a recovery phase. The starch water application has successfully mitigated the acute stress observed in p2 and p4.
vision_report.anomalies: None; all visual changes are consistent with the documented human intervention.
vision_report.narrative_description: The garden has responded positively to the recent starch water application. The plants, particularly p2 and p4, show improved turgor. p1 and p3 remain stable. The white residue on the soil is a confirmed result of the user's care and is not a fungal or mineral anomaly.
